mission.title: opentelemetry-collector: Improve error message for prometheus scrape failure
mission.description: Improve error message for prometheus scrape failure. This issue affects 23+ users.

steps.title: Identify opentelemetry-collector troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your opentelemetry-collector deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector -n opentelemetry-collector --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-collector configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-collector configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-collector
```
If a prometheus receiver, fails to scrape an endpoint, the output shows something like:

```
2021-01-12T21:11:10.496Z	warn	internal/metricsbuilder.go:104	Failed to scrape Prometheus endpoint	{"component_kind": "receiver", "component_type":

steps.title: Apply the fix for Improve error message for prometheus scrape failure
steps.description:
> any idea how to wait for the subsequent debug log

 in https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/68871bdd7db82d0d495f886c558f8ea6005ef2d0/receiver/prometheusreceiver/internal/logger.go#L46, we get key-value pairs from go-kit (used by Prometheus) for each log message, which
```yaml
2021-01-12T21:11:10.496Z	warn	internal/metricsbuilder.go:104	Failed to scrape Prometheus endpoint	{"component_kind": "receiver", "component_type": "prometheus", "component_name": "prometheus", "scrape_timestamp": 1610485870495, "target_labels": "map[instance:aks-agentpool-30099824-vmss000002 job:cadvisor]"}
```

steps.title: Confirm Improve error message for prometheus scrape… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector -n opentelemetry-collector --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-collector --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
> any idea how to wait for the subsequent debug log

 in https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/68871bdd7db82d0d495f886c558f8ea6005ef2d0/receiver/prometheusreceiver/internal/logger.go#L46, we get key-value pairs from go-kit (used by Prometheus) for each log message, which gets parsed and forwarded to zap logger.

You may change the code to catch specific errors there (e.g.
codeSnippets: 2021-01-12T21:11:10.496Z	warn	internal/metricsbuilder.go:104	Failed to scrape Prometheus endpoint	{"component_kind": "receiver", "component_type": "prometheus", "component_name": "prometheus", "scrape_timestamp": 1610485870495, "target_labels": "map[instance:aks-agentpool-30099824-vmss000002 job:cadvisor]"}

kubernetes_sd_configs:
            - role: node
            tls_config:
              ca_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt
              insecure_skip_verify: false
            bearer_token_file: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
            relabel_configs:
              - action: labelmap
                regex: __meta_kubernetes_node_label_(.+)
                # Only for Kubernetes ^1.7.3.
                # See: https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/2916
              - target_label: __address__
                replacement: kubernetes.default.svc:443
              - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_node_name]
                regex: (.+)
                target_label: __metrics_path__
                replacement: /api/v1/nodes/${1}/prox

receivers:
  prometheus:
    config:
      scrape_configs:
        - job_name: 'my-job'
          static_configs:
            - targets: ['localhost:9090']  # Replace with your actual target
          params:
            match[]:
              - '{__name__!~"up"}'  # Exclude metrics with the name "up"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-collector installed or the issue environment reproducible.
